failure: hardware-boundary: 6 legs, 6 Metal OOMs (kIOGPUCommandBufferCallbackErrorOutOfMemory) on a 16GB M1 Pro; do NOT resume on this hardware — see dogfood/distiller/EXPERIMENT-LOG.md 2026-06-12 and recipes/03-tool-result-distiller.md

resume_history.note: leg1 CRASHED at ~iter 1-20: Metal OOM (kIOGPUCommandBufferCallbackErrorOutOfMemory) at batch 2 x seqlen 4096 under external memory pressure. Leg2 = fresh start, micro-batch 1 + grad-accum 4, seqlen cap 3072 (32 train / 5 valid over-length records DROPPED, not truncated), clear-cache-threshold 2GB.

leg2 ALSO Metal-OOM'd (crashed process went 'stuck' holding ~5GB; machine chronically at ceiling from interactive workload — 108MB unused even idle). Leg3: seqlen 2048, train 514->365 records (<=1850 tokens; BELOW the 500-pair sizing minimum — accepted deviation, reserve short blobs available for a more-data round if the bar misses).

leg3 Metal-OOM at seqlen 2048/batch 1 — third OOM; root cause is machine memory availability (~100MB unused, 7GB compressor from interactive workload), not config. Parked pending a memory window; relaunch command unchanged in state.json.

leg4: user quit Chrome; next-server + Pencil stopped (authorized); two Metal-zombie trainers (UE state) still resident; --num-layers 8 (365 examples, near the <300 heuristic boundary) + cache threshold tightened to 1GB.

leg4 OOM despite minimal config (2B/batch1/seq2048/layers8) on a machine that trained 4B@12.4GB successfully — diagnosis: two UE-state Metal zombie processes from earlier crashes are pinning GPU state; REBOOT REQUIRED. Relaunch = the leg4 command in this file; everything resumes from disk.
leg5: post-reboot relaunch, leg4 config verbatim; zombies cleared, wired mem 1.9GB at launch.
leg5 OOM'd on the clean post-reboot machine — zombie theory disproved. Died at the iter-1 val pass (16 val batches, val loss 1.079 computed, then Metal OOM before training iter 1).

leg6: grad-accum removed entirely (batch 1, no accumulation, seqlen 2048, num-layers 8) — the minimal possible config. Metal OOM / exit 137, again at the iter-1 val pass, val loss 1.079 identical to leg5.

RUN CLOSED 2026-06-12: hardware boundary accepted after 6 OOMs across progressively minimal configs, two of them on a freshly rebooted machine. Diagnosis: activation memory scales with ACTUAL sequence length — this dataset's records run up to 1,850 tokens, vs the ~100-400-token records of the Level 2 triage run that trained a LARGER 4B model at 12.4GB on this same machine. 16GB is insufficient for this sequence-length profile; requirement recorded as 32GB+ (or a cloud backend). Pre-registered bar remains logged and UNCONSUMED — binding on any future training run against this dataset.
